LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CLAIMANTS/THIRD PARTIES ANTHONY CICCOLINI & JOSEPHINE CICCOLINI'S MOTION FOR ORDER DIRECTING CLERK TO HONOR LEVY AND DISBURSE $10,000.00 DEPOSIT IS DENIED. BECAUSE THE CICCOLINIS DID NOT FILE A PROOF OF SERVICE OF RENEWAL NOTICE UNTIL MARCH 14, 2013, THE WRIT OF EXECUTION FILED ON JANUARY 23, 2013 IN CASE #881041 IS INVALID AND OF NO FORCE AND EFFECT. THE NOTICE OF LIEN FILED IN CASE # 500974 ON JANUARY 30, 2013 IS LIKEWISE INVALID AND OF NO FORCE AND EFFECT BUT WAS TERMINATED ON MARCH 13, 2013. THE RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT IS VALID, AND THESE ORDERS ARE WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE CICCOLINIS RIGHT TO EXECUTE ON IT. NOTHING IN THE STATUTES OR CASE LAW REQUIRES CONTEMPORANEOUS SERVICE OF THE RENEWAL NOTICE ON THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. MR. GOODMAN TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. Notice Of Motion For Order Directing The Clerk'S Office Ot Honor Notice Of Lien And Disburse Funds Subject To The Lien;
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was unconstested. CLAIMANT ANTHONY CICCOLINI'S Motion For Order Directing The Clerk's Office To Honor Notice Of Lien And Disburse Funds Subject To The Lien is Denied. Pursuant to CCP 708.410, judgment creditor Ciccolini seeks to recover the $10,000 vexatious litigant bond that was posted by Mr.Grassi in this action, and now exonerated and discharged by Court order filed January 25, 2013. However, CCP 708.410 is not applicable to this situation. It permits a judgment creditor to obtain a lien on "any cause of action of such judgment debtor for money or property" or "the rights of such judgment debtor to money or property under any judgment subsequently procured in the action or proceeding." (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Appearances:
Martin D. Goodman, Esq. of the Law Offices of Martin D. Goodman, Tel. 415.397.7956, for claimants and moving parties;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CLAIMANTs/THIRD PARTIES ANTHONY CICCOLINI & JOSEPHINE CICCOLINI's Motion For Order Directing Clerk To Honor Levy And Disburse $10,000.00 Deposit is denied. Because the Ciccolinis did not file a proof of service of renewal notice until March 14, 2013, the writ of execution filed on January 23, 2013 in case #881041 is invalid and of no force and effect. The notice of lien filed in case # 500974 on January 30, 2013 is likewise invalid and of no force and effect but was terminated on March 13, 2013. The renewal of judgment is valid, and these orders are without prejudice to the Ciccolinis right to execute on it. Nothing in the statutes or case law requires contemporaneous service of the renewal notice on the judgment debtor. Mr. Goodman to prepare an order. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Appearances:
David Grassi, Tel. 415.552.4125, Plaintiff and moving party appearing on his own behalf
Justin S. Draa, Esq. Of Trepel Greenfield Sullivan & Draa, LLP, Tel. 408.995.5600, appearing for defendant and opposing party via Court Call.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DAVID GRASSI's Motion To Compel Defendant Julian Signes' Answers At Deposition And Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents is denied. Defendant Signes has already been deposed by Plaintiff for six hours in the presence of a court-appointed discovery referee in this limited civil case in which Plaintiff's complaint seeks seven hundred fifty dollars ($750.00). The evidence shows that the documents responsive to the request for production accompanying Plaintiff's notice of Defendant's deposition have been produced. Plaintiff's motion is brought without substantial justification. Sanctions in the amount of $3,000.00 are awarded against Plaintiff. See Do v. Superior Court (2003) 109 Cal. App. 4th 1210, 1218 (holding that discovery sanctions may be awarded in favor of the prevailing party in a discovery dispute even where the prevailing party is represented by counsel on a pro-bono basis). Plaintiff shall pay defendant Julien Signes the sum of $3,000.00 within 10 days of entry of the order. (Prevailing party to submit a form of order.) Pro Tem Judge: Ernst Halperin, Reporter: Lana L. Loper, CSR # 9667 (Tel. 818.430.9151)
